seoTitle: How to See Edit History in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: View who changed what in a Google Doc using Version History, name milestone saves, and restore earlier document states when needed.
intro: Auditing team contributions or undoing an accidental change is much easier once you know how Version History works. This guide covers opening the timeline, reading color-coded contributor edits, and restoring a previous version.

steps.heading: Open Version History
steps.body: Click File, hover over Version History, and select 'See Version History' to open the timeline panel on the right.

steps.heading: Browse saved timestamps
steps.body: Click on individual entries in the list to see how the document looked at that point in time.

steps.heading: Review contributor edits
steps.body: Check the color-coded highlights to identify which team member added or removed specific text.

steps.heading: Name an important version
steps.body: Click the three-dot menu next to a save point and select 'Name this version' to mark a milestone for later reference.

steps.heading: Restore a previous version
steps.body: Click 'Restore this version' at the top of the page and confirm to reset the document to that historical state.

faq.question: Does restoring an old version delete recent changes permanently?
faq.answer: No, restoring creates a new version entry, so the version you moved away from is still available in the history.

faq.question: Can I see edit history on the mobile app?
faq.answer: Yes, a simplified version history view is available through the mobile app's menu.

faq.question: How far back does Google Docs keep version history?
faq.answer: Google generally retains a long history, though very old granular versions may eventually be consolidated.

faq.question: Can I turn off the 'Show Changes' visual markup?
faq.answer: Yes, there's a checkbox at the bottom of the panel to toggle strikeouts and highlights on or off.
